intent: Mislabeled web docroot: httpd_t denied read+getattr on a default_t file (very common real denial when content is dropped into a path that did not inherit httpd_sys_content_t). Maps to one clean narrow allow.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic).

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: EQUAL in this case - default_t has no narrowing refpolicy interface, so audit2allow -R emitted the SAME raw allow as -N (no macro substitution fired). This is a realistic data point: not every denial has an over-broad macro to refuse. expected.te equals both. The narrow contract still holds trivially (exact perm set, no padding, no attribute, no unrelated type). Note: the canonical real fix is a relabel (restorecon / semanage fcontext to httpd_sys_content_t), not an allow at all - triage should suggest the allow but a relabel caveat is the better operator advice.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl that assumes -R always over-broadens would invent a macro-vs-raw narrowing diff here and over-state its value; in THIS scenario -R and -N are identical, so the real triage value is (a) emitting the exact { read getattr } set and (b) noting the underlying problem is a mislabel (restorecon to httpd_sys_content_t) rather than blindly suggesting a permanent allow on default_t.
